title: Basso profundo

infobox.value: * [Chest voice](/wiki/Chest_voice "Chest voice") * [Clef](/wiki/Clef "Clef") * [Extension](/wiki/Extension_(music) "Extension (music)") * [Head voice](/wiki/Head_voice "Head voice") * [Passaggio](/wiki/Passaggio "Passaggio") * [Pedal tone](/wiki/Pedal_tone "Pedal tone") * [Organ registration](/wiki/Registration_(organ) "Registration (organ)") * [Tessitura](/wiki/Tessitura "Tessitura") * [Timbre](/wiki/Timbre "Timbre") * [Vocal range](/wiki/Vocal_range "Vocal range") * [Vocal weight](/wiki/Vocal_weight "Vocal weight")
(Italian: [[ˈbasso proˈfondo]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Italian "Help:IPA/Italian"), "deep bass") is the lowest [bass](/wiki/Bass_(voice_type) "Bass (voice type)") voice type.

Owen Jander; Lionel Sawkins; [J. B. Steane](/wiki/J._B._Steane "J. B. Steane"); [Elizabeth Forbes](/wiki/Elizabeth_Forbes_(musicologist) "Elizabeth Forbes (musicologist)"). L. Macy (ed.). ["Bass"](http://www.grovemusic.com). [Grove Music Online](/wiki/Grove_Dictionary_of_Music_and_Musicians "Grove Dictionary of Music and Musicians"). Paris. p. 66. "... des *Basse-Contres* les plus graves de toutes les Voix, qui chantent la *Basse* sous la *Basse* même, & qu'il ne faut pas confondre avec les *Contre-basses*, qui sont des Instrumens."

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Basso profundo](/w/index.php?title=Basso_profundo&redirect=no "Basso profundo"))

Vocal range

| [Voice type](/wiki/Voice_type "Voice type") |
| --- |
| * [Soprano](/wiki/Soprano "Soprano") * [Mezzo-soprano](/wiki/Mezzo-soprano "Mezzo-soprano") * [Contralto](/wiki/Contralto "Contralto")  ---  * [Countertenor](/wiki/Countertenor "Countertenor") * [Tenor](/wiki/Tenor "Tenor") * [Baritone](/wiki/Baritone "Baritone") * [Bass](/wiki/Bass_(voice_type) "Bass (voice type)") |
| * [v](/wiki/Template:Voice_type "Template:Voice type") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Voice_type "Template talk:Voice type") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Voice_type "Special:EditPage/Template:Voice type") |

**Basso profondo** (Italian: [[ˈbasso proˈfondo]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Italian "Help:IPA/Italian"), "deep bass") is the lowest [bass](/wiki/Bass_(voice_type) "Bass (voice type)") voice type.

While *[The New Grove Dictionary of Opera](/wiki/The_New_Grove_Dictionary_of_Opera "The New Grove Dictionary of Opera")* defines a typical bass as having a range that extends downward to the second E below middle C ([E2](/wiki/Scientific_pitch_notation "Scientific pitch notation")),[[1]](#cite_note-Grove1-1) operatic bassi [profondi](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/profondo "wikt:profondo") can be called on to sing low C ([C2](/wiki/Scientific_pitch_notation "Scientific pitch notation")), as in the role of Baron Ochs in *[Der Rosenkavalier](/wiki/Der_Rosenkavalier "Der Rosenkavalier")*. Often [choral](/wiki/Choir "Choir") composers make use of lower notes, such as G1 or even F1; in such rare cases the choir relies on exceptionally deep-ranged bassi profondi termed **octavists**, who may sing an [octave](/wiki/Octave "Octave") below the bass part.

Bass singer [Tim Storms](/wiki/Tim_Storms "Tim Storms") holds the [Guinness World Record](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records") for the "lowest note produced by a human".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Definition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basso_profondo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Definition")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b2/Basso_profondo_voice_range_on_keyboard.svg/500px-Basso_profondo_voice_range_on_keyboard.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Basso_profondo_voice_range_on_keyboard.svg)

Basso profondo voice range (C2–C4) indicated on piano keyboard in green with dot marking middle C

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/23/Bass_C_to_F.svg/250px-Bass_C_to_F.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Bass_C_to_F.svg)

Range of a basso profondo according to the Italian definition ranging from C2 to F4

According to [Rousseau](/wiki/Jean-Jacques_Rousseau "Jean-Jacques Rousseau") (1775): "Basse-contres – the most profound of all voices, singing lower than the bass like a [double bass](/wiki/Double_bass "Double bass"), and should not be confused with [contrabasses](/wiki/Contrabass "Contrabass"), which are instruments."[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Octavist

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basso_profondo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Octavist")]

An octavist (German: Oktavist) is an exceptionally deep-ranged basso profondo, especially typical of [Russian Orthodox](/wiki/Russian_Orthodox_Church "Russian Orthodox Church") [choral music](/wiki/Choir "Choir"). This [voice type](/wiki/Voice_type "Voice type") has a vocal range which extends down to A1 (an [octave](/wiki/Octave "Octave") below the baritone range) and sometimes to F1 (an octave below the [bass staff](/wiki/Bass_staff "Bass staff")) with the extreme lows for octavists, such as [Mikhail Zlatopolsky](/wiki/Mikhail_Zlatopolsky "Mikhail Zlatopolsky") or Alexander Ort, reaching C1.[[4]](#cite_note-rubass-4)

Slavic choral composers sometimes make use of lower notes such as B♭1 as in [Rachmaninoff](/wiki/Sergei_Rachmaninoff "Sergei Rachmaninoff")'s *[All-Night Vigil](/wiki/All-Night_Vigil_(Rachmaninoff) "All-Night Vigil (Rachmaninoff)")*, G♯1 in "The Twelve Brigands", G1 in "Ne otverzhi mene" by [Pavel Chesnokov](/wiki/Pavel_Chesnokov "Pavel Chesnokov"), or F1 in "Kheruvimskaya pesn" ("Song of Cherubim") by [Krzysztof Penderecki](/wiki/Krzysztof_Penderecki "Krzysztof Penderecki"), although such notes sometimes also appear in repertoire by non-Slavic composers (e.g. B♭1 appears in [Gustav Mahler](/wiki/Gustav_Mahler "Gustav Mahler")'s [Second](/wiki/Symphony_No._2_(Mahler) "Symphony No. 2 (Mahler)") and [Eighth](/wiki/Symphony_No._8_(Mahler) "Symphony No. 8 (Mahler)") Symphonies).

In popular culture several [a capella](/wiki/A_capella "A capella") groups have bass singers with an octavist range, such as [Home Free](/wiki/Home_Free_(group) "Home Free (group)")'s Tim Foust, [VoicePlay](/wiki/VoicePlay "VoicePlay")'s Geoff Castellucci, and [Avi Kaplan](/wiki/Avi_Kaplan "Avi Kaplan") formerly of [Pentatonix](/wiki/Pentatonix "Pentatonix"), as well as Tony-nominated Broadway performer [Patrick Page](/wiki/Patrick_Page "Patrick Page"), each of these being able to reach down to a G1 or in some cases even lower, with Castellucci able to hit a B0 using [subharmonic singing](/wiki/Undertone_series "Undertone series") and Foust able to hit a G0 using [growl](/wiki/Death_growl "Death growl").

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basso_profondo&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: See also")]
